title: Phanera pterocalyx
intro: Phanera pterocalyx, also known as the "Indian gentian," is a medicinal plant species that belongs to the family Sapindaceae. It is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Phanera pterocalyx include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Phanera pterocalyx is commonly used to make herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, as well as being processed into tablets and capsules for modern use.
taxonomy: Phanera pterocalyx is classified within the family Anacardiaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the cashew family. This classification places Phanera pterocalyx among other related species such as cashews and poison ivy. The genus Phanera is a group of flowering plants native to Asia and is often characterized by its unique seeds and fruits.
